lesson_title: Type deduction with pointers, references, and const

cards.text: When using {{c1::auto}}, top-level const and references are {{c2::dropped}}. Use {{c3::auto&}} to deduce a reference type and {{c4::const auto&}} for const reference.
cards.source: 12.14 - Type deduction with pointers, references, and const

cards.question: What type is deduced for x?
cards.explanation: auto drops references and top-level const. Use auto& to preserve reference.
cards.source: 12.14 - Type deduction with pointers, references, and const

cards.question: What type is deduced for x?
cards.answer: int (not const int). auto drops top-level const.
cards.explanation: To preserve const, use const auto x = value;
cards.source: 12.14 - Type deduction with pointers, references, and const

cards.text: Use {{c1::auto&}} when you want to work with the actual object (not a copy) and {{c2::const auto&}} when you want read-only access without copying.
cards.source: 12.14 - Type deduction with pointers, references, and const

cards.front: How does auto handle pointers differently from references?
cards.back: auto preserves pointers (int* becomes auto = int*) but drops references (int& becomes auto = int). This is because * is part of the type, but & is not.
cards.source: 12.14 - Type deduction with pointers, references, and const

cards.explanation:
Key rules for auto:
  • auto drops references - use auto& to keep
  • auto drops top-level const - use const auto
  • auto preserves pointers and low-level const
  • const auto& for read-only without copy
  • auto& for modifiable reference
examples: int& ref = x; auto a = ref;      // int (drops &)
const int c = 5; auto b = c;     // int (drops const)
int* ptr = &x; auto d = ptr;     // int* (keeps *)
const auto& f = x;               // const int& (explicit)
cards.source: 12.14 - Type deduction with pointers, references, and const

cards.text: To preserve constness with auto, use {{c1::const auto}} or {{c2::const auto&}}. Plain auto {{c3::drops}} top-level const.
cards.source: 12.14 - Type deduction with pointers, references, and const

cards.text: Use {{c1::auto&}} to deduce a reference type and {{c2::const auto&}} for a const reference. Plain auto {{c3::drops}} the reference.
cards.source: 12.14 - Type deduction with pointers, references, and const
